rationale: My round-1 nit is fully resolved. The reword 'byte-superset'/'substring SUPERSET' -> 'substring-set superset' is applied in BOTH sites: gate.go:265 and panel_decision_test.go:434. Both reworded lines are pure ASCII (LC_ALL=C non-ASCII scan flags only pre-existing doc glyphs on other lines; no gofmt doc-comment corruption). No stale 'byte-superset'/'substring SUPERSET' phrasing remains anywhere. The wording is accurate: leg-9.5's message carries all of leg-10's pinned substrings plus more. VoteDecision lockstep is UNTOUCHED: git diff 4dfebbcb..a2f2451 -- tally.go is EMPTY; the identical UnresolvedVerdicts()-nonempty check sits in both PanelGateDecision (gate.go:272) and VoteDecision (tally.go:229). Substring-set superset invariant holds: go test ./internal/panel green (sub-threshold fixtures pass). Mutation-verify (leg 9.5 disabled) now FAILS the two-RC row with 'message missing revA/revB' -- revA/revB appear only via %s, confirming the row genuinely discriminates. build/gofmt/vet all clean in /tmp. Worktree left clean at a2f2451.

findings.summary: Round-1 O2 nit (comment should read 'substring-set superset', not 'byte-superset') corrected in both gate.go:265 and panel_decision_test.go:434; accurate and plain-ASCII, no gofmt corruption.

VoteDecision lockstep re-confirmed: tally.go byte-identical to round-1 (empty delta diff); twin UnresolvedVerdicts check present in both funcs. Substring-set superset invariant holds (internal/panel tests green; mutation-verify confirms fixed two-RC row discriminates via revA/revB).
